question: List the geography IDs for households where the completion status is 'Located' and the new household split number is greater than zero, grouped by their geography ID.
sql: SELECT T1.GEOGRAPHY_ID FROM FACT_HOUSEHOLD_CORE AS T1 JOIN DIM_HOUSEHOLD_STATUS AS T2 ON T1.HOUSEHOLD_STATUS_ID = T2.HOUSEHOLD_STATUS_ID WHERE T2.COMPLETION_STATUS = 'Located' AND T1.NEW_HOUSEHOLD_SPLIT_NUMBER > 0 GROUP BY T1.GEOGRAPHY_ID;

question: List the geography IDs for areas in the state of 'Maharashtra', grouped by geography ID, where there is at least one household with a new household split number greater than zero and at least one household with a completion status of 'Located', ordered by geography ID.
sql: SELECT T1.GEOGRAPHY_ID FROM FACT_HOUSEHOLD_CORE AS T1 JOIN DIM_GEOGRAPHY_HIERARCHY AS T2 ON T1.GEOGRAPHY_ID = T2.GEOGRAPHY_ID JOIN DIM_HOUSEHOLD_STATUS AS T3 ON T1.HOUSEHOLD_STATUS_ID = T3.HOUSEHOLD_STATUS_ID WHERE T2.STATE_CODE = 'Maharashtra' GROUP BY T1.GEOGRAPHY_ID HAVING SUM(CASE WHEN T1.NEW_HOUSEHOLD_SPLIT_NUMBER > 0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) > 0 AND SUM(CASE WHEN T3.COMPLETION_STATUS = 'Located' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) > 0 ORDER BY T1.GEOGRAPHY_ID ASC

question: List the PSU codes, ordered alphabetically, for those PSU codes that have at least one household with a split number greater than zero and contain both at least one household with a completion status of 'Located' and at least one household with a completion status of 'Moved out'.
sql: SELECT T1.PSU_CODE FROM DIM_GEOGRAPHY_HIERARCHY AS T1 JOIN FACT_HOUSEHOLD_CORE AS T2 ON T1.GEOGRAPHY_ID = T2.GEOGRAPHY_ID JOIN DIM_HOUSEHOLD_STATUS AS T3 ON T2.HOUSEHOLD_STATUS_ID = T3.HOUSEHOLD_STATUS_ID WHERE T2.HOUSEHOLD_SPLIT_NUMBER > 0 GROUP BY T1.PSU_CODE HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN T3.COMPLETION_STATUS = 'Located' THEN T2.HOUSEHOLD_ID END) > 0 AND COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN T3.COMPLETION_STATUS = 'Moved out' THEN T2.HOUSEHOLD_ID END) > 0 ORDER BY T1.PSU_CODE ASC

sql: SELECT T1.PSU_CODE FROM DIM_GEOGRAPHY_HIERARCHY AS T1 JOIN FACT_HOUSEHOLD_CORE AS T2 ON T1.GEOGRAPHY_ID = T2.GEOGRAPHY_ID JOIN DIM_HOUSEHOLD_STATUS AS T3 ON T2.HOUSEHOLD_STATUS_ID = T3.HOUSEHOLD_STATUS_ID WHERE T2.HOUSEHOLD_NUMBER > 1 GROUP BY T1.PSU_CODE HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN T3.COMPLETION_STATUS = 'Located' THEN T2.HOUSEHOLD_ID END) > 0 AND COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN T3.COMPLETION_STATUS = 'Non-contact' THEN T2.HOUSEHOLD_ID END) > 0 ORDER BY T1.PSU_CODE ASC

question: List the household number and the PSU code for all records where the new household split number is not null and the state code is 'Maharashtra', grouped by household number and PSU code, ensuring that the count of household IDs is at least one, and order the results by PSU code in ascending order.

question: List the PSU codes from the geography hierarchy for locations in the state of Maharashtra where the maximum new household split number is greater than 1 and there is at least one household with a status ID of 1.

question: List the PSU codes from the geography hierarchy for locations in the state of 'Maharashtra' where there is at least one household with a new household split number greater than one and where no households have a completion status of 'Moved out', ordered alphabetically by the PSU code.
sql: SELECT T1.PSU_CODE FROM DIM_GEOGRAPHY_HIERARCHY AS T1 JOIN FACT_HOUSEHOLD_CORE AS T2 ON T1.GEOGRAPHY_ID = T2.GEOGRAPHY_ID JOIN DIM_HOUSEHOLD_STATUS AS T3 ON T2.HOUSEHOLD_STATUS_ID = T3.HOUSEHOLD_STATUS_ID WHERE T1.STATE_CODE = 'Maharashtra' GROUP BY T1.PSU_CODE HAVING SUM(CASE WHEN T2.NEW_HOUSEHOLD_SPLIT_NUMBER > 1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) > 0 AND SUM(CASE WHEN T3.COMPLETION_STATUS = 'Moved out' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) = 0 ORDER BY T1.PSU_CODE ASC

question: List the PSU codes for the state of Rajasthan, grouped by PSU code, where more than one household has a split number greater than zero and no households have a completion status of 'Non-contact', ordered by the PSU code.

question: List the PSU codes from the geography hierarchy for locations in the state of 'Andhra Pradesh' where the total count of households with a new household split number greater than 1 is more than 2, and where there are zero households with a completion status of 'All dead', ordered alphabetically by the PSU code.
sql: SELECT T1.PSU_CODE FROM DIM_GEOGRAPHY_HIERARCHY AS T1 JOIN FACT_HOUSEHOLD_CORE AS T2 ON T1.GEOGRAPHY_ID = T2.GEOGRAPHY_ID JOIN DIM_HOUSEHOLD_STATUS AS T3 ON T2.HOUSEHOLD_STATUS_ID = T3.HOUSEHOLD_STATUS_ID WHERE T1.STATE_CODE = 'Andhra Pradesh' GROUP BY T1.PSU_CODE HAVING SUM(CASE WHEN T2.NEW_HOUSEHOLD_SPLIT_NUMBER > 1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) > 2 AND SUM(CASE WHEN T3.COMPLETION_STATUS = 'All dead' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) = 0 ORDER BY T1.PSU_CODE ASC

question: List the PSU codes, ordered alphabetically, for all geographic areas where there is at least one household record that meets any of the following criteria: the household split number is greater than 0, the new household split number is greater than 1, or the other completion status is specified and not unknown.
sql: SELECT T1.PSU_CODE FROM DIM_GEOGRAPHY_HIERARCHY AS T1 JOIN FACT_HOUSEHOLD_CORE AS T2 ON T1.GEOGRAPHY_ID = T2.GEOGRAPHY_ID JOIN DIM_HOUSEHOLD_STATUS AS T3 ON T2.HOUSEHOLD_STATUS_ID = T3.HOUSEHOLD_STATUS_ID WHERE T2.HOUSEHOLD_SPLIT_NUMBER > 0 OR T2.NEW_HOUSEHOLD_SPLIT_NUMBER > 1 OR (T3.COMPLETION_STATUS_OTHER IS NOT NULL AND T3.COMPLETION_STATUS_OTHER <> 'unknown') GROUP BY T1.PSU_CODE HAVING COUNT(T2.HOUSEHOLD_ID) >= 1 ORDER BY T1.PSU_CODE ASC

question: List the PSU codes for geographic areas where there are at least 2 household numbers, considering only households where the household split number is greater than 0, the new household split number is greater than 0, or the other completion status is known and not null, and order the results by the PSU code.
sql: SELECT T1.PSU_CODE FROM DIM_GEOGRAPHY_HIERARCHY AS T1 JOIN FACT_HOUSEHOLD_CORE AS T2 ON T1.GEOGRAPHY_ID = T2.GEOGRAPHY_ID JOIN DIM_HOUSEHOLD_STATUS AS T3 ON T2.HOUSEHOLD_STATUS_ID = T3.HOUSEHOLD_STATUS_ID WHERE T2.HOUSEHOLD_SPLIT_NUMBER > 0 OR T2.NEW_HOUSEHOLD_SPLIT_NUMBER > 0 OR (T3.COMPLETION_STATUS_OTHER IS NOT NULL AND T3.COMPLETION_STATUS_OTHER <> 'unknown') GROUP BY T1.PSU_CODE HAVING COUNT(T2.HOUSEHOLD_NUMBER) >= 2 ORDER BY T1.PSU_CODE ASC

question: List the person IDs for individuals who are at least 46 years old and have a marital status code of 'Widowed', grouped by their person ID and age, provided they have at least one associated household tracking record, and sort the results by age in descending order.

question: For households that have at least one recorded current location, list the household ID, the person's age, the demographic code ID, and the relationship to the head of the household, sorted by household ID in ascending order and then by age in descending order.
sql: SELECT T1.HOUSEHOLD_ID, T2.AGE, T2.DEMOGRAPHIC_CODE_ID, T3.RELATIONSHIP_TO_HEAD FROM FACT_HOUSEHOLD_CORE AS T1 JOIN FACT_PERSON_CORE AS T2 ON T1.HOUSEHOLD_ID = T2.HOUSEHOLD_ID JOIN DIM_PERSON_STATUS AS T3 ON T2.PERSON_STATUS_ID = T3.PERSON_STATUS_ID WHERE T1.HOUSEHOLD_ID IN (SELECT HOUSEHOLD_ID FROM FACT_PERSON_CORE GROUP BY HOUSEHOLD_ID HAVING COUNT(CURRENT_LOCATION_ID) > 0) ORDER BY T1.HOUSEHOLD_ID ASC, T2.AGE DESC

question: List the person ID and person number for individuals who have at least one migrant record, specifically filtering for those who were not married when they migrated or whose employment activity status is 'Salaried', grouped by their person ID and person number, and sorted by person number in descending order.
sql: SELECT T1.PERSON_ID, T1.PERSON_NUMBER FROM FACT_PERSON_CORE AS T1 JOIN FACT_MIGRANT_PERSONAL AS T2 ON T1.PERSON_ID = T2.PERSON_ID LEFT JOIN FACT_MIGRANT_EMPLOYMENT AS T3 ON T2.MIGRANT_ID = T3.MIGRANT_ID LEFT JOIN DIM_EMPLOYMENT_STATUS AS T4 ON T3.EMPLOYMENT_STATUS_ID = T4.EMPLOYMENT_STATUS_ID WHERE T2.MARRIED_WHEN_MIGRATED_INDICATOR = 'No' OR T4.ACTIVITY_STATUS = 'Salaried' GROUP BY T1.PERSON_ID, T1.PERSON_NUMBER HAVING COUNT(T2.MIGRANT_ID) >= 1 ORDER BY T1.PERSON_NUMBER DESC

question: List the person ID and age for individuals who were not married when they migrated or whose employment activity status is 'Salaried', ensuring they have at least one migrant record, and order the results by age in descending order.
sql: SELECT T1.PERSON_ID, T1.AGE FROM FACT_PERSON_CORE AS T1 JOIN FACT_MIGRANT_PERSONAL AS T2 ON T1.PERSON_ID = T2.PERSON_ID LEFT JOIN FACT_MIGRANT_EMPLOYMENT AS T3 ON T2.MIGRANT_ID = T3.MIGRANT_ID LEFT JOIN DIM_EMPLOYMENT_STATUS AS T4 ON T3.EMPLOYMENT_STATUS_ID = T4.EMPLOYMENT_STATUS_ID WHERE T2.MARRIED_WHEN_MIGRATED_INDICATOR = 'No' OR T4.ACTIVITY_STATUS = 'Salaried' GROUP BY T1.PERSON_ID, T1.AGE HAVING COUNT(T2.MIGRANT_ID) >= 1 ORDER BY T1.AGE DESC

question: List the person ID and the household ID for individuals who have at least one migrant record, where the individual either was not married when they migrated or has an employment activity status of 'Salaried', grouped by their person and household identifiers and ordered by household ID in descending order.
sql: SELECT T1.PERSON_ID, T1.HOUSEHOLD_ID FROM FACT_PERSON_CORE AS T1 JOIN FACT_MIGRANT_PERSONAL AS T2 ON T1.PERSON_ID = T2.PERSON_ID LEFT JOIN FACT_MIGRANT_EMPLOYMENT AS T3 ON T2.MIGRANT_ID = T3.MIGRANT_ID LEFT JOIN DIM_EMPLOYMENT_STATUS AS T4 ON T3.EMPLOYMENT_STATUS_ID = T4.EMPLOYMENT_STATUS_ID WHERE T2.MARRIED_WHEN_MIGRATED_INDICATOR = 'No' OR T4.ACTIVITY_STATUS = 'Salaried' GROUP BY T1.PERSON_ID, T1.HOUSEHOLD_ID HAVING COUNT(T2.MIGRANT_ID) >= 1 ORDER BY T1.HOUSEHOLD_ID DESC

question: List the migrant ID and the marital status code at the time of migration for migrants who have at least one employment record, ordered by their education code at migration in descending order, while also accounting for their education level in the grouping.
sql: SELECT T2.MIGRANT_ID, T2.MARITAL_STATUS_AT_MIGRATION_CODE FROM FACT_PERSON_CORE AS T1 JOIN FACT_MIGRANT_PERSONAL AS T2 ON T1.PERSON_ID = T2.PERSON_ID JOIN FACT_MIGRANT_EMPLOYMENT AS T3 ON T2.MIGRANT_ID = T3.MIGRANT_ID GROUP BY T2.MIGRANT_ID, T2.MARITAL_STATUS_AT_MIGRATION_CODE, T2.EDUCATION_AT_MIGRATION_CODE HAVING COUNT(T3.MIGRANT_EMPLOYMENT_ID) >= 1 ORDER BY T2.EDUCATION_AT_MIGRATION_CODE DESC

question: List the migrant ID and the marital status at migration code for individuals who were not married when they migrated, grouped by their migrant ID, marital status at migration code, and education at migration code, provided they have at least one recorded occupation, and sort the results by their education at migration code in descending order.
sql: SELECT T2.MIGRANT_ID, T2.MARITAL_STATUS_AT_MIGRATION_CODE FROM FACT_PERSON_CORE AS T1 JOIN FACT_MIGRANT_PERSONAL AS T2 ON T1.PERSON_ID = T2.PERSON_ID JOIN FACT_MIGRANT_EMPLOYMENT AS T3 ON T2.MIGRANT_ID = T3.MIGRANT_ID WHERE T2.MARRIED_WHEN_MIGRATED_INDICATOR = 'No' GROUP BY T2.MIGRANT_ID, T2.MARITAL_STATUS_AT_MIGRATION_CODE, T2.EDUCATION_AT_MIGRATION_CODE HAVING COUNT(T3.OCCUPATION_ID) >= 1 ORDER BY T2.EDUCATION_AT_MIGRATION_CODE DESC

question: List the migrant ID and the marital status code at the time of migration for individuals aged 21, 22, or 46, grouped by their migrant ID, marital status code, and education code at migration, provided they have at least one recorded employment status, and sort the results by the education code at migration in descending order.
sql: SELECT T2.MIGRANT_ID, T2.MARITAL_STATUS_AT_MIGRATION_CODE FROM FACT_PERSON_CORE AS T1 JOIN FACT_MIGRANT_PERSONAL AS T2 ON T1.PERSON_ID = T2.PERSON_ID JOIN FACT_MIGRANT_EMPLOYMENT AS T3 ON T2.MIGRANT_ID = T3.MIGRANT_ID WHERE T1.AGE IN ('21', '22', '46') GROUP BY T2.MIGRANT_ID, T2.MARITAL_STATUS_AT_MIGRATION_CODE, T2.EDUCATION_AT_MIGRATION_CODE HAVING COUNT(T3.EMPLOYMENT_STATUS_ID) >= 1 ORDER BY T2.EDUCATION_AT_MIGRATION_CODE DESC

sql: SELECT T1.EDUCATION_AT_MIGRATION_CODE, T3.ACTIVITY_STATUS, T3.EMPLOYMENT_TYPE FROM FACT_MIGRANT_PERSONAL AS T1 JOIN FACT_MIGRANT_EMPLOYMENT AS T2 ON T1.MIGRANT_ID = T2.MIGRANT_ID JOIN DIM_EMPLOYMENT_STATUS AS T3 ON T2.EMPLOYMENT_STATUS_ID = T3.EMPLOYMENT_STATUS_ID WHERE T3.ACTIVITY_STATUS = 'Salaried' AND T1.MARRIED_WHEN_MIGRATED_INDICATOR = 'No' GROUP BY T1.EDUCATION_AT_MIGRATION_CODE, T3.ACTIVITY_STATUS, T3.EMPLOYMENT_TYPE HAVING COUNT(T1.MIGRANT_ID) >= 2 ORDER BY T1.EDUCATION_AT_MIGRATION_CODE ASC

question: List the education at migration code, the activity status, and the employment type for records where either the employment type is 'Self employed' or the education at migration code is 'Secondary', ensuring at least one migrant is counted for each group and ordering the results by the education at migration code.
sql: SELECT T1.EDUCATION_AT_MIGRATION_CODE, T3.ACTIVITY_STATUS, T3.EMPLOYMENT_TYPE FROM FACT_MIGRANT_PERSONAL AS T1 JOIN FACT_MIGRANT_EMPLOYMENT AS T2 ON T1.MIGRANT_ID = T2.MIGRANT_ID JOIN DIM_EMPLOYMENT_STATUS AS T3 ON T2.EMPLOYMENT_STATUS_ID = T3.EMPLOYMENT_STATUS_ID WHERE T3.EMPLOYMENT_TYPE = 'Self employed' OR T1.EDUCATION_AT_MIGRATION_CODE = 'Secondary' GROUP BY T1.EDUCATION_AT_MIGRATION_CODE, T3.ACTIVITY_STATUS, T3.EMPLOYMENT_TYPE HAVING COUNT(T1.MIGRANT_ID) >= 1 ORDER BY T1.EDUCATION_AT_MIGRATION_CODE ASC

question: List the person IDs for individuals who have a person ID of 1 or greater and whose activity status is recorded as 'Salaried'.
sql: SELECT T1.PERSON_ID FROM FACT_MIGRANT_PERSONAL AS T1 JOIN FACT_MIGRANT_EMPLOYMENT AS T2 ON T1.MIGRANT_ID = T2.MIGRANT_ID JOIN DIM_EMPLOYMENT_STATUS AS T3 ON T2.EMPLOYMENT_STATUS_ID = T3.EMPLOYMENT_STATUS_ID WHERE T3.ACTIVITY_STATUS = 'Salaried' AND T1.PERSON_ID >= 1;

question: List the migrant IDs for all migrants whose migrant ID is greater than or equal to 2 and whose activity status is recorded as 'Salaried'.
sql: SELECT T1.MIGRANT_ID FROM FACT_MIGRANT_PERSONAL AS T1 JOIN FACT_MIGRANT_EMPLOYMENT AS T2 ON T1.MIGRANT_ID = T2.MIGRANT_ID JOIN DIM_EMPLOYMENT_STATUS AS T3 ON T2.EMPLOYMENT_STATUS_ID = T3.EMPLOYMENT_STATUS_ID WHERE T3.ACTIVITY_STATUS = 'Salaried' AND T1.MIGRANT_ID >= 2;

question: List the person IDs for individuals whose activity status is 'Salaried' and who have a person ID of 3 or greater.
sql: SELECT T1.PERSON_ID FROM FACT_MIGRANT_PERSONAL AS T1 JOIN FACT_MIGRANT_EMPLOYMENT AS T2 ON T1.MIGRANT_ID = T2.MIGRANT_ID JOIN DIM_EMPLOYMENT_STATUS AS T3 ON T2.EMPLOYMENT_STATUS_ID = T3.EMPLOYMENT_STATUS_ID WHERE T3.ACTIVITY_STATUS = 'Salaried' AND T1.PERSON_ID >= 3;

question: List the person IDs for individuals who are at least 21 years old and have an activity status of 'Salaried', ordered by their person ID.
sql: SELECT T1.PERSON_ID FROM FACT_PERSON_CORE AS T1 JOIN FACT_MIGRANT_PERSONAL AS T2 ON T1.PERSON_ID = T2.PERSON_ID JOIN FACT_MIGRANT_EMPLOYMENT AS T3 ON T2.MIGRANT_ID = T3.MIGRANT_ID JOIN DIM_EMPLOYMENT_STATUS AS T4 ON T3.EMPLOYMENT_STATUS_ID = T4.EMPLOYMENT_STATUS_ID WHERE T4.ACTIVITY_STATUS = 'Salaried' AND T1.AGE >= 21 ORDER BY T1.PERSON_ID

question: List the person numbers for individuals who are at least 22 years old and whose activity status is recorded as 'Salaried', sorted by their person number.
sql: SELECT T1.PERSON_NUMBER FROM FACT_PERSON_CORE AS T1 JOIN FACT_MIGRANT_PERSONAL AS T2 ON T1.PERSON_ID = T2.PERSON_ID JOIN FACT_MIGRANT_EMPLOYMENT AS T3 ON T2.MIGRANT_ID = T3.MIGRANT_ID JOIN DIM_EMPLOYMENT_STATUS AS T4 ON T3.EMPLOYMENT_STATUS_ID = T4.EMPLOYMENT_STATUS_ID WHERE T4.ACTIVITY_STATUS = 'Salaried' AND T1.AGE >= 22 ORDER BY T1.PERSON_NUMBER

question: List the household IDs, ordered by household ID, for individuals aged 46 or older who are migrants and have an employment activity status of 'Salaried'.
sql: SELECT T1.HOUSEHOLD_ID FROM FACT_PERSON_CORE AS T1 JOIN FACT_MIGRANT_PERSONAL AS T2 ON T1.PERSON_ID = T2.PERSON_ID JOIN FACT_MIGRANT_EMPLOYMENT AS T3 ON T2.MIGRANT_ID = T3.MIGRANT_ID JOIN DIM_EMPLOYMENT_STATUS AS T4 ON T3.EMPLOYMENT_STATUS_ID = T4.EMPLOYMENT_STATUS_ID WHERE T4.ACTIVITY_STATUS = 'Salaried' AND T1.AGE >= 46 ORDER BY T1.HOUSEHOLD_ID

question: List the person IDs for individuals who are at least 22 years old and have a migrant employment activity status of 'Salaried', grouped and ordered by their person ID.
sql: SELECT T1.PERSON_ID FROM FACT_PERSON_CORE AS T1 JOIN FACT_MIGRANT_PERSONAL AS T2 ON T1.PERSON_ID = T2.PERSON_ID JOIN FACT_MIGRANT_EMPLOYMENT AS T3 ON T2.MIGRANT_ID = T3.MIGRANT_ID JOIN DIM_EMPLOYMENT_STATUS AS T4 ON T3.EMPLOYMENT_STATUS_ID = T4.EMPLOYMENT_STATUS_ID WHERE T4.ACTIVITY_STATUS = 'Salaried' AND T1.PERSON_ID IN (SELECT PERSON_ID FROM FACT_PERSON_CORE WHERE AGE >= 22) GROUP BY T1.PERSON_ID ORDER BY T1.PERSON_ID ASC

question: List the person numbers for individuals who are at least 21 years old and have an activity status of 'Salaried', grouped and ordered by their person number.
sql: SELECT T1.PERSON_NUMBER FROM FACT_PERSON_CORE AS T1 JOIN FACT_MIGRANT_PERSONAL AS T2 ON T1.PERSON_ID = T2.PERSON_ID JOIN FACT_MIGRANT_EMPLOYMENT AS T3 ON T2.MIGRANT_ID = T3.MIGRANT_ID JOIN DIM_EMPLOYMENT_STATUS AS T4 ON T3.EMPLOYMENT_STATUS_ID = T4.EMPLOYMENT_STATUS_ID WHERE T4.ACTIVITY_STATUS = 'Salaried' AND T1.PERSON_NUMBER IN (SELECT PERSON_NUMBER FROM FACT_PERSON_CORE WHERE AGE >= 21) GROUP BY T1.PERSON_NUMBER ORDER BY T1.PERSON_NUMBER ASC

question: List the household IDs for households that contain at least one person aged 46 or older, specifically focusing on those where a migrant member has a salaried activity status, and order the results by the household ID.
sql: SELECT T1.HOUSEHOLD_ID FROM FACT_PERSON_CORE AS T1 JOIN FACT_MIGRANT_PERSONAL AS T2 ON T1.PERSON_ID = T2.PERSON_ID JOIN FACT_MIGRANT_EMPLOYMENT AS T3 ON T2.MIGRANT_ID = T3.MIGRANT_ID JOIN DIM_EMPLOYMENT_STATUS AS T4 ON T3.EMPLOYMENT_STATUS_ID = T4.EMPLOYMENT_STATUS_ID WHERE T4.ACTIVITY_STATUS = 'Salaried' AND T1.HOUSEHOLD_ID IN (SELECT HOUSEHOLD_ID FROM FACT_PERSON_CORE WHERE AGE >= 46) GROUP BY T1.HOUSEHOLD_ID ORDER BY T1.HOUSEHOLD_ID ASC

question: List the PSU codes and the total count of household IDs for each PSU code, showing the top 10 PSU codes with the highest number of households.
sql: SELECT T1.PSU_CODE, COUNT(T2.HOUSEHOLD_ID) FROM DIM_GEOGRAPHY_HIERARCHY AS T1 JOIN FACT_HOUSEHOLD_CORE AS T2 ON T1.GEOGRAPHY_ID = T2.GEOGRAPHY_ID GROUP BY T1.PSU_CODE ORDER BY COUNT(T2.HOUSEHOLD_ID) DESC LIMIT 10

question: List the PSU codes and the count of new household split numbers for each PSU code, ordered by the count in descending order, and show only the top 10 results.
sql: SELECT T1.PSU_CODE, COUNT(T2.NEW_HOUSEHOLD_SPLIT_NUMBER) FROM DIM_GEOGRAPHY_HIERARCHY AS T1 JOIN FACT_HOUSEHOLD_CORE AS T2 ON T1.GEOGRAPHY_ID = T2.GEOGRAPHY_ID GROUP BY T1.PSU_CODE ORDER BY COUNT(T2.NEW_HOUSEHOLD_SPLIT_NUMBER) DESC LIMIT 10

question: List the PSU codes and the total count of household IDs for each PSU code, considering only households where the household split number is 1, and show the top 10 PSU codes with the highest household counts.
sql: SELECT T1.PSU_CODE, COUNT(T2.HOUSEHOLD_ID) FROM DIM_GEOGRAPHY_HIERARCHY AS T1 JOIN FACT_HOUSEHOLD_CORE AS T2 ON T1.GEOGRAPHY_ID = T2.GEOGRAPHY_ID WHERE T2.HOUSEHOLD_SPLIT_NUMBER = 1 GROUP BY T1.PSU_CODE ORDER BY COUNT(T2.HOUSEHOLD_ID) DESC LIMIT 10;

question: List the PSU codes and the total count of household IDs for each PSU code located in the state of 'Maharashtra', ordered by the count of household IDs in descending order, and show only the top 10 results.
sql: SELECT T1.PSU_CODE, COUNT(T2.HOUSEHOLD_ID) FROM DIM_GEOGRAPHY_HIERARCHY AS T1 JOIN FACT_HOUSEHOLD_CORE AS T2 ON T1.GEOGRAPHY_ID = T2.GEOGRAPHY_ID WHERE T1.STATE_CODE = 'Maharashtra' GROUP BY T1.PSU_CODE ORDER BY COUNT(T2.HOUSEHOLD_ID) DESC LIMIT 10;

question: List the PSU codes and the count of household IDs for each PSU code, considering only households where the new household split number is 1, ordered by the count of household IDs in descending order for the top 10 results.
sql: SELECT T1.PSU_CODE, COUNT(T2.HOUSEHOLD_ID) FROM DIM_GEOGRAPHY_HIERARCHY AS T1 JOIN FACT_HOUSEHOLD_CORE AS T2 ON T1.GEOGRAPHY_ID = T2.GEOGRAPHY_ID WHERE T2.NEW_HOUSEHOLD_SPLIT_NUMBER = 1 GROUP BY T1.PSU_CODE ORDER BY COUNT(T2.HOUSEHOLD_ID) DESC LIMIT 10;

question: List the PSU codes and the total count of household IDs for each PSU code located in the state of 'Rajasthan', specifically where the household split number is not null and the count is greater than zero; provide the top 10 results ordered by the count of household IDs in descending order.
sql: SELECT T1.PSU_CODE, COUNT(T2.HOUSEHOLD_ID) AS col_2 FROM DIM_GEOGRAPHY_HIERARCHY AS T1 JOIN FACT_HOUSEHOLD_CORE AS T2 ON T1.GEOGRAPHY_ID = T2.GEOGRAPHY_ID JOIN FACT_HOUSEHOLD_TRACKING AS T3 ON T2.HOUSEHOLD_ID = T3.HOUSEHOLD_ID WHERE T2.HOUSEHOLD_SPLIT_NUMBER IS NOT NULL AND T1.STATE_CODE = 'Rajasthan' GROUP BY T1.PSU_CODE HAVING COUNT(T2.HOUSEHOLD_ID) > 0 ORDER BY col_2 DESC LIMIT 10

question: For the district with code '2', list each PSU code and the total count of household IDs, filtering for records where the new household split number is not null; only include PSU codes that have more than one household, and show the top 10 PSU codes with the highest household counts.
sql: SELECT T1.PSU_CODE, COUNT(T2.HOUSEHOLD_ID) AS col_2 FROM DIM_GEOGRAPHY_HIERARCHY AS T1 JOIN FACT_HOUSEHOLD_CORE AS T2 ON T1.GEOGRAPHY_ID = T2.GEOGRAPHY_ID JOIN FACT_HOUSEHOLD_TRACKING AS T3 ON T2.HOUSEHOLD_ID = T3.HOUSEHOLD_ID WHERE T2.NEW_HOUSEHOLD_SPLIT_NUMBER IS NOT NULL AND T1.DISTRICT_CODE = '2' GROUP BY T1.PSU_CODE HAVING COUNT(T2.HOUSEHOLD_ID) > 1 ORDER BY col_2 DESC LIMIT 10

question: For the state of Maharashtra, list the PSU codes and the count of household IDs for households where the household split number is not null, ensuring the count is greater than zero, and display the top 10 PSU codes with the highest household counts.
sql: SELECT T1.PSU_CODE, COUNT(T2.HOUSEHOLD_ID) AS col_2 FROM DIM_GEOGRAPHY_HIERARCHY AS T1 JOIN FACT_HOUSEHOLD_CORE AS T2 ON T1.GEOGRAPHY_ID = T2.GEOGRAPHY_ID JOIN FACT_HOUSEHOLD_TRACKING AS T3 ON T2.HOUSEHOLD_ID = T3.HOUSEHOLD_ID WHERE T2.HOUSEHOLD_SPLIT_NUMBER IS NOT NULL AND T1.STATE_CODE = 'Maharashtra' GROUP BY T1.PSU_CODE HAVING COUNT(T2.HOUSEHOLD_ID) > 0 ORDER BY col_2 DESC LIMIT 10

question: For the state of Rajasthan, list the state code, the completion status, and the total count of household IDs, grouped by both the state code and the completion status.
sql: SELECT T1.STATE_CODE, T2.COMPLETION_STATUS, COUNT(T3.HOUSEHOLD_ID) FROM DIM_GEOGRAPHY_HIERARCHY AS T1 JOIN FACT_HOUSEHOLD_CORE AS T3 ON T1.GEOGRAPHY_ID = T3.GEOGRAPHY_ID JOIN DIM_HOUSEHOLD_STATUS AS T2 ON T3.HOUSEHOLD_STATUS_ID = T2.HOUSEHOLD_STATUS_ID WHERE T1.STATE_CODE = 'Rajasthan' GROUP BY T1.STATE_CODE, T2.COMPLETION_STATUS ORDER BY T1.STATE_CODE;

question: For the state of Maharashtra, list the state code, the completion status, and the count of household IDs, grouped by both the state code and the completion status.
sql: SELECT T1.STATE_CODE, T2.COMPLETION_STATUS, COUNT(T3.HOUSEHOLD_ID) FROM DIM_GEOGRAPHY_HIERARCHY AS T1 JOIN FACT_HOUSEHOLD_CORE AS T3 ON T1.GEOGRAPHY_ID = T3.GEOGRAPHY_ID JOIN DIM_HOUSEHOLD_STATUS AS T2 ON T3.HOUSEHOLD_STATUS_ID = T2.HOUSEHOLD_STATUS_ID WHERE T1.STATE_CODE = 'Maharashtra' GROUP BY T1.STATE_CODE, T2.COMPLETION_STATUS ORDER BY T1.STATE_CODE;

question: For each state code and completion status, what is the state code, the completion status, and the total count of household IDs for households where the completion status is 'Located'?
sql: SELECT T1.STATE_CODE, T2.COMPLETION_STATUS, COUNT(T3.HOUSEHOLD_ID) FROM DIM_GEOGRAPHY_HIERARCHY AS T1 JOIN FACT_HOUSEHOLD_CORE AS T3 ON T1.GEOGRAPHY_ID = T3.GEOGRAPHY_ID JOIN DIM_HOUSEHOLD_STATUS AS T2 ON T3.HOUSEHOLD_STATUS_ID = T2.HOUSEHOLD_STATUS_ID WHERE T2.COMPLETION_STATUS = 'Located' GROUP BY T1.STATE_CODE, T2.COMPLETION_STATUS ORDER BY T1.STATE_CODE;

question: For the state of Rajasthan, list the state code, the completion status, and the total count of household IDs, grouped by both the state code and the completion status, ordered alphabetically by state code.
sql: SELECT T1.STATE_CODE, T2.COMPLETION_STATUS, COUNT(T3.HOUSEHOLD_ID) FROM DIM_GEOGRAPHY_HIERARCHY AS T1 JOIN FACT_HOUSEHOLD_CORE AS T3 ON T1.GEOGRAPHY_ID = T3.GEOGRAPHY_ID JOIN DIM_HOUSEHOLD_STATUS AS T2 ON T3.HOUSEHOLD_STATUS_ID = T2.HOUSEHOLD_STATUS_ID WHERE T1.STATE_CODE = 'Rajasthan' GROUP BY T1.STATE_CODE, T2.COMPLETION_STATUS ORDER BY T1.STATE_CODE ASC

question: Show the state code, the completion status, and the count of household IDs for records where the completion status is 'Located', grouped by both the state code and the completion status, ordered alphabetically by state code.
sql: SELECT T1.STATE_CODE, T2.COMPLETION_STATUS, COUNT(T3.HOUSEHOLD_ID) FROM DIM_GEOGRAPHY_HIERARCHY AS T1 JOIN FACT_HOUSEHOLD_CORE AS T3 ON T1.GEOGRAPHY_ID = T3.GEOGRAPHY_ID JOIN DIM_HOUSEHOLD_STATUS AS T2 ON T3.HOUSEHOLD_STATUS_ID = T2.HOUSEHOLD_STATUS_ID WHERE T2.COMPLETION_STATUS = 'Located' GROUP BY T1.STATE_CODE, T2.COMPLETION_STATUS ORDER BY T1.STATE_CODE ASC

question: For the state code 'Maharashtra' and the completion status 'Moved out', list the state code, the completion status, and the count of household IDs, grouped by both state code and completion status, ordered by state code.
sql: SELECT T1.STATE_CODE, T2.COMPLETION_STATUS, COUNT(T3.HOUSEHOLD_ID) FROM DIM_GEOGRAPHY_HIERARCHY AS T1 JOIN FACT_HOUSEHOLD_CORE AS T3 ON T1.GEOGRAPHY_ID = T3.GEOGRAPHY_ID JOIN DIM_HOUSEHOLD_STATUS AS T2 ON T3.HOUSEHOLD_STATUS_ID = T2.HOUSEHOLD_STATUS_ID WHERE T1.STATE_CODE = 'Maharashtra' AND T2.COMPLETION_STATUS = 'Moved out' GROUP BY T1.STATE_CODE, T2.COMPLETION_STATUS ORDER BY T1.STATE_CODE ASC

question: For the states of Maharashtra and Rajasthan, and considering only households with a split number of 0 or 1, list each state code and completion status along with the total count of household IDs, ensuring only groups with at least one household are shown, ordered by state code and then by the count in descending order.
sql: SELECT T1.STATE_CODE, T2.COMPLETION_STATUS, COUNT(T3.HOUSEHOLD_ID) FROM DIM_GEOGRAPHY_HIERARCHY AS T1 JOIN FACT_HOUSEHOLD_CORE AS T3 ON T1.GEOGRAPHY_ID = T3.GEOGRAPHY_ID JOIN DIM_HOUSEHOLD_STATUS AS T2 ON T3.HOUSEHOLD_STATUS_ID = T2.HOUSEHOLD_STATUS_ID WHERE T1.STATE_CODE IN ('Maharashtra', 'Rajasthan') AND T3.HOUSEHOLD_SPLIT_NUMBER IN (0, 1) GROUP BY T1.STATE_CODE, T2.COMPLETION_STATUS HAVING COUNT(T3.HOUSEHOLD_ID) > 0 ORDER BY T1.STATE_CODE ASC, COUNT(T3.HOUSEHOLD_ID) DESC

question: For districts with codes '2', '9', or '20', list each state code and the completion status, along with the total count of household IDs, specifically for records where the completion status is 'Located' and the household count is greater than one, ordered by state code and then by the count in descending order.
sql: SELECT T1.STATE_CODE, T2.COMPLETION_STATUS, COUNT(T3.HOUSEHOLD_ID) FROM DIM_GEOGRAPHY_HIERARCHY AS T1 JOIN FACT_HOUSEHOLD_CORE AS T3 ON T1.GEOGRAPHY_ID = T3.GEOGRAPHY_ID JOIN DIM_HOUSEHOLD_STATUS AS T2 ON T3.HOUSEHOLD_STATUS_ID = T2.HOUSEHOLD_STATUS_ID WHERE T2.COMPLETION_STATUS = 'Located' AND T1.DISTRICT_CODE IN ('2', '9', '20') GROUP BY T1.STATE_CODE, T2.COMPLETION_STATUS HAVING COUNT(T3.HOUSEHOLD_ID) > 1 ORDER BY T1.STATE_CODE ASC, COUNT(T3.HOUSEHOLD_ID) DESC

question: For the states of 'Andhra Pradesh' and 'Jammu & Kashmir', list the state code, the completion status, and the total count of household IDs, ensuring that only groups with at least one household are included, and order the results by state code in ascending order and household count in descending order.
sql: SELECT T1.STATE_CODE, T2.COMPLETION_STATUS, COUNT(T3.HOUSEHOLD_ID) FROM DIM_GEOGRAPHY_HIERARCHY AS T1 JOIN FACT_HOUSEHOLD_CORE AS T3 ON T1.GEOGRAPHY_ID = T3.GEOGRAPHY_ID JOIN DIM_HOUSEHOLD_STATUS AS T2 ON T3.HOUSEHOLD_STATUS_ID = T2.HOUSEHOLD_STATUS_ID WHERE T1.STATE_CODE = 'Andhra Pradesh' OR T1.STATE_CODE = 'Jammu & Kashmir' GROUP BY T1.STATE_CODE, T2.COMPLETION_STATUS HAVING COUNT(T3.HOUSEHOLD_ID) >= 1 ORDER BY T1.STATE_CODE ASC, COUNT(T3.HOUSEHOLD_ID) DESC

question: Show all columns from the household tracking table and the household status table for records where the completion status is 'Located' and the other completion status details are not 'unknown'.
sql: SELECT * FROM FACT_HOUSEHOLD_TRACKING JOIN DIM_HOUSEHOLD_STATUS ON FACT_HOUSEHOLD_TRACKING.HOUSEHOLD_ID = DIM_HOUSEHOLD_STATUS.HOUSEHOLD_STATUS_ID WHERE DIM_HOUSEHOLD_STATUS.COMPLETION_STATUS = 'Located' AND DIM_HOUSEHOLD_STATUS.COMPLETION_STATUS_OTHER != 'unknown';

question: Show all columns from the household core table and the household status table for records where the completion status is 'Located' and the other completion status is not 'unknown'.
sql: SELECT * FROM FACT_HOUSEHOLD_CORE JOIN DIM_HOUSEHOLD_STATUS ON FACT_HOUSEHOLD_CORE.HOUSEHOLD_STATUS_ID = DIM_HOUSEHOLD_STATUS.HOUSEHOLD_STATUS_ID WHERE DIM_HOUSEHOLD_STATUS.COMPLETION_STATUS = 'Located' AND DIM_HOUSEHOLD_STATUS.COMPLETION_STATUS_OTHER != 'unknown';

question: List each household ID and the count of household tracking IDs for records where the completion status is 'Located' and the other completion status is 'unknown', grouped by the household ID and ordered by the count in descending order.
sql: SELECT T1.HOUSEHOLD_ID, COUNT(T1.HOUSEHOLD_TRACKING_ID) FROM FACT_HOUSEHOLD_TRACKING AS T1 JOIN DIM_HOUSEHOLD_STATUS AS T2 ON T1.HOUSEHOLD_ID = T2.HOUSEHOLD_STATUS_ID WHERE T2.COMPLETION_STATUS = 'Located' AND T2.COMPLETION_STATUS_OTHER = 'unknown' GROUP BY T1.HOUSEHOLD_ID ORDER BY COUNT(T1.HOUSEHOLD_TRACKING_ID) DESC;

question: List each household ID along with the maximum household tracking ID for households where the completion status is 'Located' and not 'Non-contact', grouped by the household ID and ordered by the maximum tracking ID in ascending order.
sql: SELECT T1.HOUSEHOLD_ID, MAX(T1.HOUSEHOLD_TRACKING_ID) FROM FACT_HOUSEHOLD_TRACKING AS T1 JOIN DIM_HOUSEHOLD_STATUS AS T2 ON T1.HOUSEHOLD_ID = T2.HOUSEHOLD_STATUS_ID WHERE T2.COMPLETION_STATUS = 'Located' AND T2.COMPLETION_STATUS != 'Non-contact' GROUP BY T1.HOUSEHOLD_ID ORDER BY MAX(T1.HOUSEHOLD_TRACKING_ID) ASC;

question: For households with a completion status of 'Located' and not 'Not found', list each household ID along with its minimum household tracking ID, grouped by the household ID and ordered by the minimum tracking ID in descending order.
sql: SELECT T1.HOUSEHOLD_ID, MIN(T1.HOUSEHOLD_TRACKING_ID) FROM FACT_HOUSEHOLD_TRACKING AS T1 JOIN DIM_HOUSEHOLD_STATUS AS T2 ON T1.HOUSEHOLD_ID = T2.HOUSEHOLD_STATUS_ID WHERE T2.COMPLETION_STATUS = 'Located' AND T2.COMPLETION_STATUS != 'Not found' GROUP BY T1.HOUSEHOLD_ID ORDER BY MIN(T1.HOUSEHOLD_TRACKING_ID) DESC;

question: List the household status ID and the completion status for households where the same address indicator is 'Yes' and there are no records of that household having a 'No' or 'Unknown' same address indicator; group the results by the household status ID and completion status for groups with at least one household, and order the list by household status ID in ascending order.
sql: SELECT T2.HOUSEHOLD_STATUS_ID, T2.COMPLETION_STATUS FROM FACT_HOUSEHOLD_TRACKING AS T0 JOIN FACT_HOUSEHOLD_CORE AS T1 ON T0.HOUSEHOLD_ID = T1.HOUSEHOLD_ID JOIN DIM_HOUSEHOLD_STATUS AS T2 ON T1.HOUSEHOLD_STATUS_ID = T2.HOUSEHOLD_STATUS_ID WHERE T0.SAME_ADDRESS_INDICATOR = 'Yes' AND T0.HOUSEHOLD_ID NOT IN (SELECT HOUSEHOLD_ID FROM FACT_HOUSEHOLD_TRACKING WHERE SAME_ADDRESS_INDICATOR = 'No' OR SAME_ADDRESS_INDICATOR = 'Unknown') GROUP BY T2.HOUSEHOLD_STATUS_ID, T2.COMPLETION_STATUS HAVING COUNT(T0.HOUSEHOLD_ID) >= 1 ORDER BY T2.HOUSEHOLD_STATUS_ID ASC

question: List the household status ID and the completion status for households where the completion status is 'Located', excluding any households that have moved out or were not found according to their address indicator. Only include groups of these statuses that have a count of at least 2 household IDs, and order the results by the household status ID in ascending order.
sql: SELECT T2.HOUSEHOLD_STATUS_ID, T2.COMPLETION_STATUS FROM FACT_HOUSEHOLD_TRACKING AS T0 JOIN FACT_HOUSEHOLD_CORE AS T1 ON T0.HOUSEHOLD_ID = T1.HOUSEHOLD_ID JOIN DIM_HOUSEHOLD_STATUS AS T2 ON T1.HOUSEHOLD_STATUS_ID = T2.HOUSEHOLD_STATUS_ID WHERE T2.COMPLETION_STATUS = 'Located' AND T0.HOUSEHOLD_ID NOT IN (SELECT HOUSEHOLD_ID FROM FACT_HOUSEHOLD_TRACKING WHERE SAME_ADDRESS_INDICATOR = 'Moved out' OR SAME_ADDRESS_INDICATOR = 'Not found') GROUP BY T2.HOUSEHOLD_STATUS_ID, T2.COMPLETION_STATUS HAVING COUNT(T0.HOUSEHOLD_ID) >= 2 ORDER BY T2.HOUSEHOLD_STATUS_ID ASC

question: List the household status ID and the completion status for households where the other completion status is 'unknown', excluding any households that were marked as 'Non-contact' or 'All dead' in the tracking records; group the results by the household status ID and completion status for those with at least one tracking record, and sort the list by the household status ID in ascending order.
sql: SELECT T2.HOUSEHOLD_STATUS_ID, T2.COMPLETION_STATUS FROM FACT_HOUSEHOLD_TRACKING AS T0 JOIN FACT_HOUSEHOLD_CORE AS T1 ON T0.HOUSEHOLD_ID = T1.HOUSEHOLD_ID JOIN DIM_HOUSEHOLD_STATUS AS T2 ON T1.HOUSEHOLD_STATUS_ID = T2.HOUSEHOLD_STATUS_ID WHERE T2.COMPLETION_STATUS_OTHER = 'unknown' AND T0.HOUSEHOLD_ID NOT IN (SELECT HOUSEHOLD_ID FROM FACT_HOUSEHOLD_TRACKING WHERE SAME_ADDRESS_INDICATOR = 'Non-contact' OR SAME_ADDRESS_INDICATOR = 'All dead') GROUP BY T2.HOUSEHOLD_STATUS_ID, T2.COMPLETION_STATUS HAVING COUNT(T0.HOUSEHOLD_ID) >= 1 ORDER BY T2.HOUSEHOLD_STATUS_ID ASC

question: List the PSU codes from the geography hierarchy for households where the completion status is 'Located' and the household split number is greater than zero, grouped by their PSU code.
sql: SELECT T1.PSU_CODE FROM DIM_GEOGRAPHY_HIERARCHY AS T1 JOIN FACT_HOUSEHOLD_CORE AS T2 ON T1.GEOGRAPHY_ID = T2.GEOGRAPHY_ID JOIN DIM_HOUSEHOLD_STATUS AS T3 ON T2.HOUSEHOLD_STATUS_ID = T3.HOUSEHOLD_STATUS_ID WHERE T3.COMPLETION_STATUS = 'Located' AND T2.HOUSEHOLD_SPLIT_NUMBER > 0 GROUP BY T1.PSU_CODE;

question: List the PSU codes from the geography hierarchy for households where the completion status is 'Located' and the new household split number is greater than zero, grouped by the PSU code.
sql: SELECT T1.PSU_CODE FROM DIM_GEOGRAPHY_HIERARCHY AS T1 JOIN FACT_HOUSEHOLD_CORE AS T2 ON T1.GEOGRAPHY_ID = T2.GEOGRAPHY_ID JOIN DIM_HOUSEHOLD_STATUS AS T3 ON T2.HOUSEHOLD_STATUS_ID = T3.HOUSEHOLD_STATUS_ID WHERE T3.COMPLETION_STATUS = 'Located' AND T2.NEW_HOUSEHOLD_SPLIT_NUMBER > 0 GROUP BY T1.PSU_CODE;

question: List the PSU codes from the geography hierarchy for households where the completion status is 'Located' and the household number is greater than 1, grouped by the PSU code.

question: List the occupation descriptions for individuals who were not employed at the time of their death, grouped by their occupation, where the maximum number of years since death for any person in that group is greater than three.
sql: SELECT T1.OCCUPATION_DESCRIPTION FROM DIM_OCCUPATION_CODES AS T1 JOIN FACT_DECEASED_OCCUPATION AS T2 ON T1.OCCUPATION_ID = T2.OCCUPATION_ID JOIN FACT_DECEASED_PERSON_DETAILS AS T3 ON T2.DECEASED_ID = T3.DECEASED_ID WHERE T2.EMPLOYED_AT_DEATH = 'No' GROUP BY T1.OCCUPATION_DESCRIPTION HAVING MAX(T3.YEARS_SINCE_DEATH) > '3';

question: List the person IDs for individuals who were either married at the time of migration or have a salaried activity status, ensuring they have at least one employment record, and order the results by person ID.
sql: SELECT T1.PERSON_ID FROM FACT_MIGRANT_PERSONAL AS T1 JOIN FACT_MIGRANT_EMPLOYMENT AS T2 ON T1.MIGRANT_ID = T2.MIGRANT_ID JOIN DIM_EMPLOYMENT_STATUS AS T3 ON T2.EMPLOYMENT_STATUS_ID = T3.EMPLOYMENT_STATUS_ID WHERE T1.MARITAL_STATUS_AT_MIGRATION_CODE = 'Married' OR T3.ACTIVITY_STATUS = 'Salaried' GROUP BY T1.PERSON_ID HAVING COUNT(T2.MIGRANT_EMPLOYMENT_ID) >= 1 ORDER BY T1.PERSON_ID ASC

question: List the migrant IDs for individuals who either have a secondary education level at the time of migration or have a salaried activity status, ensuring they have at least one recorded occupation, and order the results by their migrant ID.
sql: SELECT T1.MIGRANT_ID FROM FACT_MIGRANT_PERSONAL AS T1 JOIN FACT_MIGRANT_EMPLOYMENT AS T2 ON T1.MIGRANT_ID = T2.MIGRANT_ID JOIN DIM_EMPLOYMENT_STATUS AS T3 ON T2.EMPLOYMENT_STATUS_ID = T3.EMPLOYMENT_STATUS_ID WHERE T1.EDUCATION_AT_MIGRATION_CODE = 'Secondary' OR T3.ACTIVITY_STATUS = 'Salaried' GROUP BY T1.MIGRANT_ID HAVING COUNT(T2.OCCUPATION_ID) >= 1 ORDER BY T1.MIGRANT_ID ASC

question: List the person IDs for individuals who were either unmarried at the time of migration or have a salaried activity status, ensuring each person has at least one recorded employment status, and order the results by person ID.
sql: SELECT T1.PERSON_ID FROM FACT_MIGRANT_PERSONAL AS T1 JOIN FACT_MIGRANT_EMPLOYMENT AS T2 ON T1.MIGRANT_ID = T2.MIGRANT_ID JOIN DIM_EMPLOYMENT_STATUS AS T3 ON T2.EMPLOYMENT_STATUS_ID = T3.EMPLOYMENT_STATUS_ID WHERE T1.MARITAL_STATUS_AT_MIGRATION_CODE = 'Unmarried' OR T3.ACTIVITY_STATUS = 'Salaried' GROUP BY T1.PERSON_ID HAVING COUNT(T2.EMPLOYMENT_STATUS_ID) >= 1 ORDER BY T1.PERSON_ID ASC

question: Provide the migrant ID, the marital status code at the time of migration, and the education code at the time of migration for all records where the marital status code at migration is 'Married'.
sql: SELECT MIGRANT_ID, MARITAL_STATUS_AT_MIGRATION_CODE, EDUCATION_AT_MIGRATION_CODE FROM FACT_MIGRANT_PERSONAL WHERE MARITAL_STATUS_AT_MIGRATION_CODE = 'Married';

question: List the person ID, the marital status code at the time of migration, and the education code at the time of migration for all records where the education at migration code is 'Secondary'.
sql: SELECT PERSON_ID, MARITAL_STATUS_AT_MIGRATION_CODE, EDUCATION_AT_MIGRATION_CODE FROM FACT_MIGRANT_PERSONAL WHERE EDUCATION_AT_MIGRATION_CODE = 'Secondary';

question: List the migrant ID, the person ID, and the marital status code at the time of migration for all records where the education code at migration is '1st class'.
sql: SELECT MIGRANT_ID, PERSON_ID, MARITAL_STATUS_AT_MIGRATION_CODE FROM FACT_MIGRANT_PERSONAL WHERE EDUCATION_AT_MIGRATION_CODE = '1st class';

question: List the person ID, person number, and age from the core person records, along with the marital status at migration code, for all individuals whose marital status at migration code is 'Married', ordered by their person ID.
sql: SELECT T1.PERSON_ID, T1.PERSON_NUMBER, T1.AGE, T2.MARITAL_STATUS_AT_MIGRATION_CODE FROM FACT_PERSON_CORE AS T1 JOIN FACT_MIGRANT_PERSONAL AS T2 ON T1.PERSON_ID = T2.PERSON_ID WHERE T2.MARITAL_STATUS_AT_MIGRATION_CODE = 'Married' ORDER BY T1.PERSON_ID

question: List the person ID, household ID, age, and the education code at the time of migration for individuals whose marital status at migration was 'Married', ordered by their person ID.
sql: SELECT T1.PERSON_ID, T1.HOUSEHOLD_ID, T1.AGE, T2.EDUCATION_AT_MIGRATION_CODE FROM FACT_PERSON_CORE AS T1 JOIN FACT_MIGRANT_PERSONAL AS T2 ON T1.PERSON_ID = T2.PERSON_ID WHERE T2.MARITAL_STATUS_AT_MIGRATION_CODE = 'Married' ORDER BY T1.PERSON_ID

question: List the person ID, person number, and demographic code ID, along with the indicator for whether they were married when they migrated, for all individuals whose marital status at migration code is 'Married', ordered by their person ID.
sql: SELECT T1.PERSON_ID, T1.PERSON_NUMBER, T1.DEMOGRAPHIC_CODE_ID, T2.MARRIED_WHEN_MIGRATED_INDICATOR FROM FACT_PERSON_CORE AS T1 JOIN FACT_MIGRANT_PERSONAL AS T2 ON T1.PERSON_ID = T2.PERSON_ID WHERE T2.MARITAL_STATUS_AT_MIGRATION_CODE = 'Married' ORDER BY T1.PERSON_ID

question: List the person number, the marital status at migration code, and the education at migration code for individuals who have a 'No' indicator for being married when they migrated, grouped by these same three fields and ordered by person number in ascending order.
sql: SELECT T1.PERSON_NUMBER, T2.MARITAL_STATUS_AT_MIGRATION_CODE, T2.EDUCATION_AT_MIGRATION_CODE FROM FACT_PERSON_CORE AS T1 JOIN FACT_MIGRANT_PERSONAL AS T2 ON T1.PERSON_ID = T2.PERSON_ID WHERE T2.MARRIED_WHEN_MIGRATED_INDICATOR = 'No' GROUP BY T1.PERSON_NUMBER, T2.MARITAL_STATUS_AT_MIGRATION_CODE, T2.EDUCATION_AT_MIGRATION_CODE ORDER BY T1.PERSON_NUMBER ASC

question: List the age, the marital status at migration code, and the education at migration code for individuals whose marital status at migration is 'Married', grouped by these three attributes and ordered by age in ascending order.
sql: SELECT T1.AGE, T2.MARITAL_STATUS_AT_MIGRATION_CODE, T2.EDUCATION_AT_MIGRATION_CODE FROM FACT_PERSON_CORE AS T1 JOIN FACT_MIGRANT_PERSONAL AS T2 ON T1.PERSON_ID = T2.PERSON_ID WHERE T2.MARITAL_STATUS_AT_MIGRATION_CODE = 'Married' GROUP BY T1.AGE, T2.MARITAL_STATUS_AT_MIGRATION_CODE, T2.EDUCATION_AT_MIGRATION_CODE ORDER BY T1.AGE ASC

question: List the person ID, the marital status code at migration, and the education code at migration for all individuals whose education code at migration is 'Secondary', grouped by these three attributes and ordered by person ID.
sql: SELECT T1.PERSON_ID, T2.MARITAL_STATUS_AT_MIGRATION_CODE, T2.EDUCATION_AT_MIGRATION_CODE FROM FACT_PERSON_CORE AS T1 JOIN FACT_MIGRANT_PERSONAL AS T2 ON T1.PERSON_ID = T2.PERSON_ID WHERE T2.EDUCATION_AT_MIGRATION_CODE = 'Secondary' GROUP BY T1.PERSON_ID, T2.MARITAL_STATUS_AT_MIGRATION_CODE, T2.EDUCATION_AT_MIGRATION_CODE ORDER BY T1.PERSON_ID ASC

question: List the person IDs for migrants who had a secondary education level at the time of migration and whose current employment activity status is not salaried.
sql: SELECT T1.PERSON_ID FROM FACT_MIGRANT_PERSONAL AS T1 JOIN FACT_MIGRANT_EMPLOYMENT AS T2 ON T1.MIGRANT_ID = T2.MIGRANT_ID JOIN DIM_EMPLOYMENT_STATUS AS T3 ON T2.EMPLOYMENT_STATUS_ID = T3.EMPLOYMENT_STATUS_ID WHERE T1.EDUCATION_AT_MIGRATION_CODE = 'Secondary' AND T3.ACTIVITY_STATUS != 'Salaried';

question: Provide the migrant IDs for individuals whose education level at the time of migration was '1st class' and whose current activity status is not 'Cultivation'.
sql: SELECT T1.MIGRANT_ID FROM FACT_MIGRANT_PERSONAL AS T1 JOIN FACT_MIGRANT_EMPLOYMENT AS T2 ON T1.MIGRANT_ID = T2.MIGRANT_ID JOIN DIM_EMPLOYMENT_STATUS AS T3 ON T2.EMPLOYMENT_STATUS_ID = T3.EMPLOYMENT_STATUS_ID WHERE T1.EDUCATION_AT_MIGRATION_CODE = '1st class' AND T3.ACTIVITY_STATUS != 'Cultivation';

question: List the person IDs for individuals whose education level at the time of migration was '8th class' and whose current activity status is not 'Nonaglab'.
sql: SELECT T1.PERSON_ID FROM FACT_MIGRANT_PERSONAL AS T1 JOIN FACT_MIGRANT_EMPLOYMENT AS T2 ON T1.MIGRANT_ID = T2.MIGRANT_ID JOIN DIM_EMPLOYMENT_STATUS AS T3 ON T2.EMPLOYMENT_STATUS_ID = T3.EMPLOYMENT_STATUS_ID WHERE T1.EDUCATION_AT_MIGRATION_CODE = '8th class' AND T3.ACTIVITY_STATUS != 'Nonaglab';

question: List the person IDs for migrants whose education level at the time of migration was 'Secondary', specifically filtering for those who have never held a 'Salaried' activity status across their employment records.
sql: SELECT T1.PERSON_ID FROM FACT_MIGRANT_PERSONAL AS T1 JOIN FACT_MIGRANT_EMPLOYMENT AS T2 ON T1.MIGRANT_ID = T2.MIGRANT_ID JOIN DIM_EMPLOYMENT_STATUS AS T3 ON T2.EMPLOYMENT_STATUS_ID = T3.EMPLOYMENT_STATUS_ID WHERE T1.EDUCATION_AT_MIGRATION_CODE = 'Secondary' GROUP BY T1.PERSON_ID HAVING SUM(CASE WHEN T3.ACTIVITY_STATUS = 'Salaried' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) = 0;

question: List the person IDs for individuals whose education level at the time of migration was '1st class', specifically filtering for those who have never had an activity status of 'Cultivation' across their employment records.
sql: SELECT T1.PERSON_ID FROM FACT_MIGRANT_PERSONAL AS T1 JOIN FACT_MIGRANT_EMPLOYMENT AS T2 ON T1.MIGRANT_ID = T2.MIGRANT_ID JOIN DIM_EMPLOYMENT_STATUS AS T3 ON T2.EMPLOYMENT_STATUS_ID = T3.EMPLOYMENT_STATUS_ID WHERE T1.EDUCATION_AT_MIGRATION_CODE = '1st class' GROUP BY T1.PERSON_ID HAVING SUM(CASE WHEN T3.ACTIVITY_STATUS = 'Cultivation' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) = 0;

question: List the person IDs for migrants whose education level at the time of migration was '8th class' and who have never held an employment activity status of 'Nonaglab'.
sql: SELECT T1.PERSON_ID FROM FACT_MIGRANT_PERSONAL AS T1 JOIN FACT_MIGRANT_EMPLOYMENT AS T2 ON T1.MIGRANT_ID = T2.MIGRANT_ID JOIN DIM_EMPLOYMENT_STATUS AS T3 ON T2.EMPLOYMENT_STATUS_ID = T3.EMPLOYMENT_STATUS_ID WHERE T1.EDUCATION_AT_MIGRATION_CODE = '8th class' GROUP BY T1.PERSON_ID HAVING SUM(CASE WHEN T3.ACTIVITY_STATUS = 'Nonaglab' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) = 0;

question: List the person IDs for individuals whose education level at the time of migration was 'Secondary', excluding those who have ever held a 'Salaried' activity status according to their migrant employment records, and group the results by person ID in ascending order.
sql: SELECT T1.PERSON_ID FROM FACT_MIGRANT_PERSONAL AS T1 JOIN FACT_PERSON_CORE AS T2 ON T1.PERSON_ID = T2.PERSON_ID WHERE T1.EDUCATION_AT_MIGRATION_CODE = 'Secondary' AND T1.PERSON_ID NOT IN ( SELECT T3.PERSON_ID FROM FACT_MIGRANT_PERSONAL AS T3 JOIN FACT_MIGRANT_EMPLOYMENT AS T4 ON T3.MIGRANT_ID = T4.MIGRANT_ID JOIN DIM_EMPLOYMENT_STATUS AS T5 ON T4.EMPLOYMENT_STATUS_ID = T5.EMPLOYMENT_STATUS_ID WHERE T5.ACTIVITY_STATUS = 'Salaried' ) GROUP BY T1.PERSON_ID ORDER BY T1.PERSON_ID ASC

question: List the person IDs for individuals whose education level at migration was '1st class', excluding those who have an employment activity status of 'Nonaglab', and show the results grouped and ordered by their person ID.
sql: SELECT T1.PERSON_ID FROM FACT_MIGRANT_PERSONAL AS T1 JOIN FACT_PERSON_CORE AS T2 ON T1.PERSON_ID = T2.PERSON_ID WHERE T1.EDUCATION_AT_MIGRATION_CODE = '1st class' AND T1.PERSON_ID NOT IN ( SELECT T3.PERSON_ID FROM FACT_MIGRANT_PERSONAL AS T3 JOIN FACT_MIGRANT_EMPLOYMENT AS T4 ON T3.MIGRANT_ID = T4.MIGRANT_ID JOIN DIM_EMPLOYMENT_STATUS AS T5 ON T4.EMPLOYMENT_STATUS_ID = T5.EMPLOYMENT_STATUS_ID WHERE T5.ACTIVITY_STATUS = 'Nonaglab' ) GROUP BY T1.PERSON_ID ORDER BY T1.PERSON_ID ASC

question: List the person IDs for individuals whose education level at the time of migration was '8th class', excluding those who have an employment activity status of 'Cultivation', and group the results by person ID sorted in ascending order.
sql: SELECT T1.PERSON_ID FROM FACT_MIGRANT_PERSONAL AS T1 JOIN FACT_PERSON_CORE AS T2 ON T1.PERSON_ID = T2.PERSON_ID WHERE T1.EDUCATION_AT_MIGRATION_CODE = '8th class' AND T1.PERSON_ID NOT IN ( SELECT T3.PERSON_ID FROM FACT_MIGRANT_PERSONAL AS T3 JOIN FACT_MIGRANT_EMPLOYMENT AS T4 ON T3.MIGRANT_ID = T4.MIGRANT_ID JOIN DIM_EMPLOYMENT_STATUS AS T5 ON T4.EMPLOYMENT_STATUS_ID = T5.EMPLOYMENT_STATUS_ID WHERE T5.ACTIVITY_STATUS = 'Cultivation' ) GROUP BY T1.PERSON_ID ORDER BY T1.PERSON_ID ASC

question: List the reason descriptions for migrants with a migrant ID of 1 or higher who have a permanently moved indicator of 'No'.
sql: SELECT T1.REASON_DESCRIPTION FROM DIM_MIGRATION_REASONS AS T1 JOIN FACT_MIGRATION_BEHAVIOR AS T2 ON T1.MIGRATION_REASON_ID = T2.MIGRATION_REASON_ID JOIN FACT_MIGRANT_PERSONAL AS T3 ON T2.MIGRANT_ID = T3.MIGRANT_ID WHERE T3.MIGRANT_ID >= 1 AND T2.PERMANENTLY_MOVED_INDICATOR = 'No';

question: List the reason descriptions for migration for individuals with a person ID of 1 or higher who have not moved permanently according to the permanently moved indicator.
sql: SELECT T1.REASON_DESCRIPTION FROM DIM_MIGRATION_REASONS AS T1 JOIN FACT_MIGRATION_BEHAVIOR AS T2 ON T1.MIGRATION_REASON_ID = T2.MIGRATION_REASON_ID JOIN FACT_MIGRANT_PERSONAL AS T3 ON T2.MIGRANT_ID = T3.MIGRANT_ID WHERE T3.PERSON_ID >= 1 AND T2.PERMANENTLY_MOVED_INDICATOR = 'No';

question: List the reason descriptions for migrants with a migrant ID of 3 or higher who have a permanently moved indicator of 'No'.
sql: SELECT T1.REASON_DESCRIPTION FROM DIM_MIGRATION_REASONS AS T1 JOIN FACT_MIGRATION_BEHAVIOR AS T2 ON T1.MIGRATION_REASON_ID = T2.MIGRATION_REASON_ID JOIN FACT_MIGRANT_PERSONAL AS T3 ON T2.MIGRANT_ID = T3.MIGRANT_ID WHERE T3.MIGRANT_ID >= 3 AND T2.PERMANENTLY_MOVED_INDICATOR = 'No';

question: List the reason descriptions for migrants with an ID of 1 or greater who have not moved permanently, grouped and ordered alphabetically by the reason description.
sql: SELECT T1.REASON_DESCRIPTION FROM DIM_MIGRATION_REASONS AS T1 JOIN FACT_MIGRATION_BEHAVIOR AS T2 ON T1.MIGRATION_REASON_ID = T2.MIGRATION_REASON_ID JOIN FACT_MIGRANT_PERSONAL AS T3 ON T2.MIGRANT_ID = T3.MIGRANT_ID WHERE T3.MIGRANT_ID >= 1 AND T2.PERMANENTLY_MOVED_INDICATOR = 'No' GROUP BY T1.REASON_DESCRIPTION ORDER BY T1.REASON_DESCRIPTION ASC

question: List the reason descriptions for migration, sorted alphabetically, for individuals with a person ID of 2 or higher who have not moved permanently, grouped by their reason description.
sql: SELECT T1.REASON_DESCRIPTION FROM DIM_MIGRATION_REASONS AS T1 JOIN FACT_MIGRATION_BEHAVIOR AS T2 ON T1.MIGRATION_REASON_ID = T2.MIGRATION_REASON_ID JOIN FACT_MIGRANT_PERSONAL AS T3 ON T2.MIGRANT_ID = T3.MIGRANT_ID WHERE T3.PERSON_ID >= 2 AND T2.PERMANENTLY_MOVED_INDICATOR = 'No' GROUP BY T1.REASON_DESCRIPTION ORDER BY T1.REASON_DESCRIPTION ASC

question: List the unique reason descriptions for migrants who have a migrant ID of 3 or higher and have not permanently moved, grouped and ordered alphabetically by the reason description.
sql: SELECT T1.REASON_DESCRIPTION FROM DIM_MIGRATION_REASONS AS T1 JOIN FACT_MIGRATION_BEHAVIOR AS T2 ON T1.MIGRATION_REASON_ID = T2.MIGRATION_REASON_ID JOIN FACT_MIGRANT_PERSONAL AS T3 ON T2.MIGRANT_ID = T3.MIGRANT_ID WHERE T3.MIGRANT_ID >= 3 AND T2.PERMANENTLY_MOVED_INDICATOR = 'No' GROUP BY T1.REASON_DESCRIPTION ORDER BY T1.REASON_DESCRIPTION ASC

question: List the reason descriptions for migration, specifically for migrants with an ID of 1 or greater, where the reason ID matches the specific migration reason ID of 1, ensuring that each reason description is associated with at least one migrant and sorted in ascending order.
sql: SELECT T1.REASON_DESCRIPTION FROM DIM_MIGRATION_REASONS AS T1 JOIN FACT_MIGRATION_BEHAVIOR AS T2 ON T1.MIGRATION_REASON_ID = T2.MIGRATION_REASON_ID JOIN FACT_MIGRANT_PERSONAL AS T3 ON T2.MIGRANT_ID = T3.MIGRANT_ID WHERE T3.MIGRANT_ID >= 1 AND T1.MIGRATION_REASON_ID IN (SELECT T4.MIGRATION_REASON_ID FROM FACT_MIGRATION_BEHAVIOR AS T4 WHERE T4.MIGRATION_REASON_ID = 1) GROUP BY T1.REASON_DESCRIPTION HAVING COUNT(T2.MIGRANT_ID) > 0 ORDER BY T1.REASON_DESCRIPTION ASC

question: List the reason descriptions for migration, ordered alphabetically, for people with a person ID of 2 or greater, specifically for those migration reasons that were also recorded for the migrant with ID 3, ensuring that at least one migration behavior record exists for each description.
sql: SELECT T1.REASON_DESCRIPTION FROM DIM_MIGRATION_REASONS AS T1 JOIN FACT_MIGRATION_BEHAVIOR AS T2 ON T1.MIGRATION_REASON_ID = T2.MIGRATION_REASON_ID JOIN FACT_MIGRANT_PERSONAL AS T3 ON T2.MIGRANT_ID = T3.MIGRANT_ID WHERE T3.PERSON_ID >= 2 AND T1.MIGRATION_REASON_ID IN (SELECT T4.MIGRATION_REASON_ID FROM FACT_MIGRATION_BEHAVIOR AS T4 WHERE T4.MIGRANT_ID = 3) GROUP BY T1.REASON_DESCRIPTION HAVING COUNT(T2.MIGRATION_BEHAVIOR_ID) > 0 ORDER BY T1.REASON_DESCRIPTION ASC

question: List the reason descriptions for migration, specifically for migrants with an ID of 3 or higher, where the migration reason ID matches any reason associated with migrant ID 5; ensure each reason description is shown only if it has at least one associated migrant record, and sort the results alphabetically by the reason description.
sql: SELECT T1.REASON_DESCRIPTION FROM DIM_MIGRATION_REASONS AS T1 JOIN FACT_MIGRATION_BEHAVIOR AS T2 ON T1.MIGRATION_REASON_ID = T2.MIGRATION_REASON_ID JOIN FACT_MIGRANT_PERSONAL AS T3 ON T2.MIGRANT_ID = T3.MIGRANT_ID WHERE T3.MIGRANT_ID >= 3 AND T1.MIGRATION_REASON_ID IN (SELECT T4.MIGRATION_REASON_ID FROM FACT_MIGRATION_BEHAVIOR AS T4 WHERE T4.MIGRANT_ID = 5) GROUP BY T1.REASON_DESCRIPTION HAVING COUNT(T2.MIGRANT_ID) > 0 ORDER BY T1.REASON_DESCRIPTION ASC

question: List the top 5 migration reason descriptions and the total count of migrant IDs for each reason, filtered for migrants whose marital status at the time of migration was 'Married', ordered by the count in descending order.
sql: SELECT T1.REASON_DESCRIPTION, COUNT(T2.MIGRANT_ID) FROM DIM_MIGRATION_REASONS AS T1 JOIN FACT_MIGRATION_BEHAVIOR AS T2 ON T1.MIGRATION_REASON_ID = T2.MIGRATION_REASON_ID JOIN FACT_MIGRANT_PERSONAL AS T3 ON T2.MIGRANT_ID = T3.MIGRANT_ID WHERE T3.MARITAL_STATUS_AT_MIGRATION_CODE = 'Married' GROUP BY T1.REASON_DESCRIPTION ORDER BY COUNT(T2.MIGRANT_ID) DESC LIMIT 5

question: For the migration reason described as 'Study', list that reason description and the total count of migrant IDs, ordered by the count in descending order, showing only the top 5 results.
sql: SELECT T1.REASON_DESCRIPTION, COUNT(T2.MIGRANT_ID) FROM DIM_MIGRATION_REASONS AS T1 JOIN FACT_MIGRATION_BEHAVIOR AS T2 ON T1.MIGRATION_REASON_ID = T2.MIGRATION_REASON_ID JOIN FACT_MIGRANT_PERSONAL AS T3 ON T2.MIGRANT_ID = T3.MIGRANT_ID WHERE T1.REASON_DESCRIPTION = 'Study' GROUP BY T1.REASON_DESCRIPTION ORDER BY COUNT(T2.MIGRANT_ID) DESC LIMIT 5

question: List the reason description and the total count of migrant IDs for each reason, specifically for migrants whose education level at the time of migration was 'Secondary', grouped by the reason description and ordered by the count in descending order for the top 5 results.

question: List the reason description and the total count of migrant IDs for each reason, specifically for migrants whose marital status at the time of migration was 'Unmarried', grouped by the reason description and ordered by the count in descending order for the top 5 results.

question: List the top 5 migration reason descriptions and the total count of migrant IDs for each reason, filtered for migrants whose marital status at the time of migration was 'Married', ordered by the count in descending order.

question: List the reason descriptions and the total count of migrant IDs for each reason, specifically for migrants who had a secondary education and were married at the time of migration, grouped by the reason description and ordered by the count in descending order for the top 5 results.
sql: SELECT T1.REASON_DESCRIPTION, COUNT(T2.MIGRANT_ID) FROM DIM_MIGRATION_REASONS AS T1 JOIN FACT_MIGRATION_BEHAVIOR AS T2 ON T1.MIGRATION_REASON_ID = T2.MIGRATION_REASON_ID JOIN FACT_MIGRANT_PERSONAL AS T3 ON T2.MIGRANT_ID = T3.MIGRANT_ID WHERE T3.EDUCATION_AT_MIGRATION_CODE = 'Secondary' AND T3.MARITAL_STATUS_AT_MIGRATION_CODE = 'Married' GROUP BY T1.REASON_DESCRIPTION ORDER BY COUNT(T2.MIGRANT_ID) DESC LIMIT 5

sql: SELECT T1.REASON_DESCRIPTION, COUNT(T2.MIGRANT_ID) FROM DIM_MIGRATION_REASONS AS T1 JOIN FACT_MIGRATION_BEHAVIOR AS T2 ON T1.MIGRATION_REASON_ID = T2.MIGRATION_REASON_ID JOIN FACT_MIGRANT_PERSONAL AS T3 ON T2.MIGRANT_ID = T3.MIGRANT_ID WHERE T2.PERMANENTLY_MOVED_INDICATOR = 'No' AND T3.MARITAL_STATUS_AT_MIGRATION_CODE = 'Married' GROUP BY T1.REASON_DESCRIPTION ORDER BY COUNT(T2.MIGRANT_ID) DESC LIMIT 5

question: List the migrant ID, the number of years since migration, and the migration reason description for individuals who have not permanently moved, ordered by the years since migration in descending order.
sql: SELECT T1.MIGRANT_ID, T1.YEARS_SINCE_MIGRATION, T2.REASON_DESCRIPTION FROM FACT_MIGRATION_BEHAVIOR AS T1 JOIN DIM_MIGRATION_REASONS AS T2 ON T1.MIGRATION_REASON_ID = T2.MIGRATION_REASON_ID WHERE T1.PERMANENTLY_MOVED_INDICATOR = 'No' ORDER BY T1.YEARS_SINCE_MIGRATION DESC

question: List the migrant ID, the frequency of visiting, and the reason description for migrants who moved 3 years ago, ordered by their migrant ID in descending order.
sql: SELECT T1.MIGRANT_ID, T1.FREQUENCY_OF_VISITING, T2.REASON_DESCRIPTION FROM FACT_MIGRATION_BEHAVIOR AS T1 JOIN DIM_MIGRATION_REASONS AS T2 ON T1.MIGRATION_REASON_ID = T2.MIGRATION_REASON_ID WHERE T1.YEARS_SINCE_MIGRATION = '3' ORDER BY T1.MIGRANT_ID DESC

question: List the migrant ID, the number of years since migration, and the migration reason description for all records where the migration reason ID is 1, sorted by the years since migration in descending order.
sql: SELECT T1.MIGRANT_ID, T1.YEARS_SINCE_MIGRATION, T2.REASON_DESCRIPTION FROM FACT_MIGRATION_BEHAVIOR AS T1 JOIN DIM_MIGRATION_REASONS AS T2 ON T1.MIGRATION_REASON_ID = T2.MIGRATION_REASON_ID WHERE T1.MIGRATION_REASON_ID = 1 ORDER BY T1.YEARS_SINCE_MIGRATION DESC

question: List the migrant ID, the education at migration code, and the years since migration for individuals who have not permanently moved, ordered by the number of years since migration in descending order.
sql: SELECT T1.MIGRANT_ID, T1.EDUCATION_AT_MIGRATION_CODE, T2.YEARS_SINCE_MIGRATION FROM FACT_MIGRANT_PERSONAL AS T1 JOIN FACT_MIGRATION_BEHAVIOR AS T2 ON T1.MIGRANT_ID = T2.MIGRANT_ID WHERE T2.PERMANENTLY_MOVED_INDICATOR = 'No' ORDER BY T2.YEARS_SINCE_MIGRATION DESC;

question: List the person ID, the education at migration code, and the migration reason ID for individuals where the years since migration is exactly 3, ordered by the years since migration in descending order.
sql: SELECT T1.PERSON_ID, T1.EDUCATION_AT_MIGRATION_CODE, T2.MIGRATION_REASON_ID FROM FACT_MIGRANT_PERSONAL AS T1 JOIN FACT_MIGRATION_BEHAVIOR AS T2 ON T1.MIGRANT_ID = T2.MIGRANT_ID WHERE T2.YEARS_SINCE_MIGRATION = '3' ORDER BY T2.YEARS_SINCE_MIGRATION DESC;

question: List the migrant ID, the marital status code at the time of migration, and the frequency of visiting for migrants who have not moved permanently, ordered by the number of years since migration in descending order.
sql: SELECT T1.MIGRANT_ID, T1.MARITAL_STATUS_AT_MIGRATION_CODE, T2.FREQUENCY_OF_VISITING FROM FACT_MIGRANT_PERSONAL AS T1 JOIN FACT_MIGRATION_BEHAVIOR AS T2 ON T1.MIGRANT_ID = T2.MIGRANT_ID WHERE T2.PERMANENTLY_MOVED_INDICATOR = 'No' ORDER BY T2.YEARS_SINCE_MIGRATION DESC;

question: List the migrant ID, the education at migration code, and the migration reason description for individuals who have not moved permanently and have been migrated for more than 3 years, ordered by the number of years since migration from most to least.
sql: SELECT T1.MIGRANT_ID, T1.EDUCATION_AT_MIGRATION_CODE, T3.REASON_DESCRIPTION FROM FACT_MIGRANT_PERSONAL AS T1 JOIN FACT_MIGRATION_BEHAVIOR AS T2 ON T1.MIGRANT_ID = T2.MIGRANT_ID JOIN DIM_MIGRATION_REASONS AS T3 ON T2.MIGRATION_REASON_ID = T3.MIGRATION_REASON_ID WHERE T2.PERMANENTLY_MOVED_INDICATOR = 'No' GROUP BY T1.MIGRANT_ID, T1.EDUCATION_AT_MIGRATION_CODE, T3.REASON_DESCRIPTION, T2.YEARS_SINCE_MIGRATION HAVING T2.YEARS_SINCE_MIGRATION > '3' ORDER BY T2.YEARS_SINCE_MIGRATION DESC

question: List the person ID, the education at migration code, and the migration reason description for individuals whose migration reason ID is 1 and who migrated more than 3 years ago, ordered by the number of years since migration from highest to lowest.
sql: SELECT T1.PERSON_ID, T1.EDUCATION_AT_MIGRATION_CODE, T3.REASON_DESCRIPTION FROM FACT_MIGRANT_PERSONAL AS T1 JOIN FACT_MIGRATION_BEHAVIOR AS T2 ON T1.MIGRANT_ID = T2.MIGRANT_ID JOIN DIM_MIGRATION_REASONS AS T3 ON T2.MIGRATION_REASON_ID = T3.MIGRATION_REASON_ID WHERE T2.MIGRATION_REASON_ID = 1 GROUP BY T1.PERSON_ID, T1.EDUCATION_AT_MIGRATION_CODE, T3.REASON_DESCRIPTION, T2.YEARS_SINCE_MIGRATION HAVING T2.YEARS_SINCE_MIGRATION > '3' ORDER BY T2.YEARS_SINCE_MIGRATION DESC

question: For individuals who were not married when they migrated and have been migrated for more than 3 years, list their migrant ID, their marital status code at the time of migration, and the description of their migration reason, ordered by the number of years since migration in descending order.
sql: SELECT T1.MIGRANT_ID, T1.MARITAL_STATUS_AT_MIGRATION_CODE, T3.REASON_DESCRIPTION FROM FACT_MIGRANT_PERSONAL AS T1 JOIN FACT_MIGRATION_BEHAVIOR AS T2 ON T1.MIGRANT_ID = T2.MIGRANT_ID JOIN DIM_MIGRATION_REASONS AS T3 ON T2.MIGRATION_REASON_ID = T3.MIGRATION_REASON_ID WHERE T1.MARRIED_WHEN_MIGRATED_INDICATOR = 'No' GROUP BY T1.MIGRANT_ID, T1.MARITAL_STATUS_AT_MIGRATION_CODE, T3.REASON_DESCRIPTION, T2.YEARS_SINCE_MIGRATION HAVING T2.YEARS_SINCE_MIGRATION > '3' ORDER BY T2.YEARS_SINCE_MIGRATION DESC

question: For each migration reason description, what is the average number of years since migration and the total count of migrant IDs?
sql: SELECT T1.REASON_DESCRIPTION, AVG(T2.YEARS_SINCE_MIGRATION), COUNT(T2.MIGRANT_ID) FROM DIM_MIGRATION_REASONS AS T1 JOIN FACT_MIGRATION_BEHAVIOR AS T2 ON T1.MIGRATION_REASON_ID = T2.MIGRATION_REASON_ID GROUP BY T1.REASON_DESCRIPTION;

question: For the migration reason described as 'Study', list that reason description along with the average number of years since migration and the total count of migration behavior IDs.
sql: SELECT T1.REASON_DESCRIPTION, AVG(T2.YEARS_SINCE_MIGRATION), COUNT(T2.MIGRATION_BEHAVIOR_ID) FROM DIM_MIGRATION_REASONS AS T1 JOIN FACT_MIGRATION_BEHAVIOR AS T2 ON T1.MIGRATION_REASON_ID = T2.MIGRATION_REASON_ID WHERE T1.REASON_DESCRIPTION = 'Study' GROUP BY T1.REASON_DESCRIPTION;

question: For individuals who have not moved permanently, list each migration reason description along with the average number of years since migration and the total count of migrant IDs for each of those reasons.
sql: SELECT T1.REASON_DESCRIPTION, AVG(T2.YEARS_SINCE_MIGRATION), COUNT(T2.MIGRANT_ID) FROM DIM_MIGRATION_REASONS AS T1 JOIN FACT_MIGRATION_BEHAVIOR AS T2 ON T1.MIGRATION_REASON_ID = T2.MIGRATION_REASON_ID WHERE T2.PERMANENTLY_MOVED_INDICATOR = 'No' GROUP BY T1.REASON_DESCRIPTION;

question: List each reason description along with the average years since migration and the total count of migrant IDs, grouped by the reason description and ordered by the average years since migration in descending order.
sql: SELECT T1.REASON_DESCRIPTION, AVG(T2.YEARS_SINCE_MIGRATION), COUNT(T2.MIGRANT_ID) FROM DIM_MIGRATION_REASONS AS T1 JOIN FACT_MIGRATION_BEHAVIOR AS T2 ON T1.MIGRATION_REASON_ID = T2.MIGRATION_REASON_ID GROUP BY T1.REASON_DESCRIPTION ORDER BY AVG(T2.YEARS_SINCE_MIGRATION) DESC;

question: For individuals who have not moved permanently, list each migration reason description along with the average number of years since migration and the total count of migrant IDs, grouped by the reason description and ordered by the average years since migration from highest to lowest.
sql: SELECT T1.REASON_DESCRIPTION, AVG(T2.YEARS_SINCE_MIGRATION), COUNT(T2.MIGRANT_ID) FROM DIM_MIGRATION_REASONS AS T1 JOIN FACT_MIGRATION_BEHAVIOR AS T2 ON T1.MIGRATION_REASON_ID = T2.MIGRATION_REASON_ID WHERE T2.PERMANENTLY_MOVED_INDICATOR = 'No' GROUP BY T1.REASON_DESCRIPTION ORDER BY AVG(T2.YEARS_SINCE_MIGRATION) DESC;

question: For migrants who were unmarried at the time of migration, list each reason description, the average number of years since migration, and the total count of migrant IDs, grouped by the reason description and ordered by the average years since migration from highest to lowest, excluding any reasons with no migrants.
sql: SELECT T1.REASON_DESCRIPTION, AVG(T2.YEARS_SINCE_MIGRATION), COUNT(T2.MIGRANT_ID) FROM DIM_MIGRATION_REASONS AS T1 JOIN FACT_MIGRATION_BEHAVIOR AS T2 ON T1.MIGRATION_REASON_ID = T2.MIGRATION_REASON_ID JOIN FACT_MIGRANT_PERSONAL AS T3 ON T2.MIGRANT_ID = T3.MIGRANT_ID WHERE T3.MARITAL_STATUS_AT_MIGRATION_CODE = 'Unmarried' GROUP BY T1.REASON_DESCRIPTION HAVING COUNT(T2.MIGRANT_ID) > 0 ORDER BY AVG(T2.YEARS_SINCE_MIGRATION) DESC

question: For migrants whose education level at the time of migration was 'Secondary', list each migration reason description along with the average number of years since migration and the total count of migrant IDs, grouped by the reason description and ordered by the average years since migration in descending order.
sql: SELECT T1.REASON_DESCRIPTION, AVG(T2.YEARS_SINCE_MIGRATION), COUNT(T2.MIGRANT_ID) FROM DIM_MIGRATION_REASONS AS T1 JOIN FACT_MIGRATION_BEHAVIOR AS T2 ON T1.MIGRATION_REASON_ID = T2.MIGRATION_REASON_ID JOIN FACT_MIGRANT_PERSONAL AS T3 ON T2.MIGRANT_ID = T3.MIGRANT_ID WHERE T3.EDUCATION_AT_MIGRATION_CODE = 'Secondary' GROUP BY T1.REASON_DESCRIPTION HAVING COUNT(T2.MIGRANT_ID) > 0 ORDER BY AVG(T2.YEARS_SINCE_MIGRATION) DESC

question: List each migration reason description along with the average years since migration and the total count of migrant IDs for those who were not married when they migrated, grouped by the reason description and ordered by the average years since migration in descending order.
sql: SELECT T1.REASON_DESCRIPTION, AVG(T2.YEARS_SINCE_MIGRATION), COUNT(T2.MIGRANT_ID) FROM DIM_MIGRATION_REASONS AS T1 JOIN FACT_MIGRATION_BEHAVIOR AS T2 ON T1.MIGRATION_REASON_ID = T2.MIGRATION_REASON_ID JOIN FACT_MIGRANT_PERSONAL AS T3 ON T2.MIGRANT_ID = T3.MIGRANT_ID WHERE T3.MARRIED_WHEN_MIGRATED_INDICATOR = 'No' GROUP BY T1.REASON_DESCRIPTION HAVING COUNT(T2.MIGRANT_ID) > 0 ORDER BY AVG(T2.YEARS_SINCE_MIGRATION) DESC
